violations.text: No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Tamales, noodles and tortitas were observed held at 117-126 in tabletop hot holding boxdegrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed and reheated during time of inspection. Unit was adjusted to a higher setting during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026

Tortillas, Manteca de cerdo, helados, choco bananas packaged and made in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients (including sub ingredients), the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2026

-2nd well of steam table was in poor repair. Well was out of service prior to inspection. -Right side hot holding display case was observed at 130 degrees Fahrenheit at the highest setting. Right side of unit was taken out of service to hold TCS foods during time of inspection. Units to be repaired/ adjusted/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026
No person in certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Raw shelled eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as manteca in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. 3 bay sink was set up during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Tamales, Atoles and Sopa were observed held at 88-117 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed and reheated during time of inspection. First 2 wells of hot holding unit were not holding proper temperature. Establishment informed to cease use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Chile Rellenos and Chicken Sandwiches were observed held at room temperature at 64 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per staff, foods were made 7 AM this morning. Time temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Eggs were observed held at 60 degrees Fahrenheit in a nonfunctional refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per staff, eggs were placed into unit at 8 AM this morning. Time temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. PIC informed that no foods are to be placed into this unit unless properly repaired/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Tortillas packaged and made in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients (including sub ingredients), the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2026
Refuse area was observed with an accumulation of nonfunctional equipment, trash, and clutter. Area to be maintained to prevent harborage conditions for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

The bakery and hot food station does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. PIC has parts, waiting on installation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

The bakery and hot food station does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination (eggs over beef). Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Various foods were observed held between 92-113 degrees Fahrenheit in the steam table and fried food hot holding unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed during time of inspection and reheated between 173-180 degrees Fahrenheit. Hot holding units were also adjusted to a higher setting to increase unit temperature. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Tortillas packaged and prepared in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC stated that food labels are in process of being made. Sign stating ingredients of product was placed with product during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025
The kitchen hood lights were not operational during time of inspection. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the dry food storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025

Establishment does not have an individual with the Rhode Island Certified Food Safety Manager License. Application provided during time of inspection. Establishment has 2 individuals with food safety certificate. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Retail food safety booklet provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025

The bakery and hot food station does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025

Raw shelled eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as pineapple filling in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025

Tamales were observed held at 128 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed during time of inspection and reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025

Shredded lettuce and crumbled cheese was observed held at 61 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed and placed into refrigeration during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025

Tortillas packaged and prepared in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ovens, retail ice cream freezer and various shelving in the kitchen have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025

The floors, ceilings and walls in the kitchen have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025
The kitchen hood lights were not operational during time of inspection. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the dry food storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2025
